section: BILINGUAL (PUNJABI IN PERSIAN CHARACTER AND URDU)—MISCELLANEOUS.—concluded.
author: Kushi Ram Arif, Lala
title: Bustan-i-Arif al-maruf Gau Mata ki Pukar
gloss: A collection of short discourses in prose and verse on certain religious and moral subjects, such as cow-protection, marriage of infant girls with old men, renunciation, etc., etc.
notes: 'The Cry of Mother Cow' — cow-protection literature from Bannu (NWFP), in Persian-script Punjabi and Urdu.

section: BILINGUAL (PUNJABI IN PERSIAN CHARACTER AND URDU)—POLITICS.
author: Mihan Singh, Baba
title: Hamari Mazhabi Ta'lim
gloss: A small tract in which the writer quotes some verses from the Granth, the Sikh scriptures, enjoining fidelity to Government and obedience to rulers.
notes: Sikh scriptural loyalism — completes the quarter's all-loyalist POLITICS sections (Urdu s2–4, this).

author: Karam Chand, B.
title: Missar ki La Ilmi ka Kachcha Chhita, Tract No. 1
gloss: Refutation of a tract entitled 'Sawami Dayanand ki Das Fash Ghaltian noted in entry No. 62 at page 44 of this catalogue.'
notes: Arya Samaj counter-refutation of the Sanatanist tract at p44 s62 — the catalog itself cross-references the polemic exchange within the same quarter: controversy in real time.

title: Mundak Opnishad
gloss: The text of the Mundak Opnishada with translation and an independent commentary by Swami Darshanand.

section: BILINGUAL (SANSKRIT IN PERSIAN CHARACTER AND URDU)—RELIGION.
title: Sandhiya
gloss: Hindu daily prayer-book with text in Sanskrit and its translation in Urdu.
notes: Sanskrit liturgy transliterated into Persian script — Hindu prayer through the Urdu reading public; 5,000 copies.

section: TRILINGUAL (ARABIC, URDU AND PUNJABI IN PERSIAN CHARACTER)—RELIGION.
author: Abdul Aziz, Maulvi
title: Faryad-i-Islam
gloss: A pamphlet in which the writer tries to show that the present degradation of the Muslims is due to their neglect in obeying their religious commandments as predicted by Muhammad quoting traditions.
notes: Dual date: 1328 Higri / 9th July 1910. Trilingual decline-and-revival lament.
